summary.intent: Iteratively improve a Claude Code session/checkpoint viewer (Gossamer) with UI improvements: redacted thinking indicators, branch selection dropdown, commit message display in checkpoints, CheckpointRow redesign, URL-encoded search, and a major two-panel layout rework for both SessionDetail and BranchLog views.
summary.outcome: All UI improvements were implemented and TypeScript builds passed cleanly. The two-panel layout was applied to both SessionDetail and BranchLog; checkpoint selection was fixed to use composite sessionId:checkpointId keys; live 'In progress' shadow sessions appear at the top of both panels.

repo: CheckpointRow is defined in SessionDetail.tsx and imported by BranchLog.tsx — it's a single shared component, so changes apply to both views automatically.
LogContent contentType field has values: text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result, redacted_thinking.
Shadow branch naming: entire/<base-commit[:7]>-<worktree-id-hash[:6]>. Each session gets its own shadow branch derived from the HEAD commit at session start, not from the working branch name.

ShadowSession.gitBranch is populated from the first log event in the JSONL that carries a gitBranch field — it is locked to the branch active at session start and won't update if user checks out a new branch mid-session.
Git log format %H%x00%B%x1e captures commit hash (before null byte) and body (before record separator) in one pass.
The entire CLI associates sessions to shadow branches via base commit hash, not branch name — there is no explicit 'this session belongs to branch X' field on the entire side.
Multiple checkpoints across different sessions can share the same checkpointId string — selection must use a composite sessionId:checkpointId key to distinguish them.

buildCheckpointGroups buckets log events by comparing event timestamps against checkpoint createdAt: an event goes into the first checkpoint whose createdAt >= the event timestamp; events after the last checkpoint go into the 'shadow' bucket.

code.finding: CheckpointRow header layout: intent/commitMessage on top, then Group with checkpointId (teal monospace), commitHash (7 chars, dimmed monospace), branch Badge.
Selection key must be `${sessionId}:${checkpointId}` not just `checkpointId` — multiple entries across different sessions can share the same checkpointId.

GET /api/branches runs `git -C <path> branch` and strips the * prefix from the current branch, returning string[]. GET /api/branch-log now also returns liveSession (the ShadowSession matching gitBranch) and commitHash per entry.
Comment explains same session appears in multiple shadow branches as transcript grows; deduplication is by blob SHA.
SessionCheckpoint and BranchLogEntry both have commitMessage: string | null and commitHash: string | null. BranchLogResponse now includes liveSession: { sessionId, repoRoot, gitBranch } | null.

workflow: When a Mantine Select dropdown was lazily populated via onDropdownOpen, the setBranches() state update fired during interaction caused a re-render that closed the dropdown. Fix: fetch eagerly on mount so no state change occurs while dropdown is open.
Express automatically decodes URL query params, but making decodeURIComponent explicit in the handler is cleaner and self-documenting when paired with explicit encodeURIComponent on the client.
TypeScript build (npm run build) was used after each set of changes to validate types before moving on — this caught issues early without needing to run the full Electron app.

friction: Branch dropdown was placed in the wrong screen twice (ActiveSessions, then Repos) before landing in the correct location (Layout.tsx header, only on /branch-log route) — three iterations required.
Edit tool failed with 'string not found' when trying to update BranchLogEntry in api.ts because SessionCheckpoint above it had already been modified, changing the surrounding context.
Mantine Select dropdown closed unexpectedly when branches were loaded lazily (onDropdownOpen) due to React re-render triggered by setState during interaction.
Multiple checkpoints sharing the same checkpointId across different sessions caused all of them to be selected simultaneously — required switching to composite key after user reported the visual bug.
open_items: The gitBranch association on ShadowSession is locked to the branch at session start — if a user checks out a different branch mid-session, the live session won't appear under the new branch in BranchLog.
The entire CLI doesn't explicitly store a working-branch-to-session mapping; Gossamer infers it from gitBranch in log events, which may be absent or stale in edge cases.
No explicit handling for the case where a shadow branch has multiple sessions — the server queries findFirst() which may not surface the most relevant live session.
The 'In progress' shadow session on BranchLog shows all uncommitted events unfiltered (no checkpoint bucketing), which differs slightly from SessionDetail's shadow bucket behavior.
